item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting take on the "only 10 percent of your brain" trope. Though a little thing that strikes me as weird, I feel like they would have installed a pacemaker on him as one of the first things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Careful what you wish for *cackle cackle cackle*

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Heh, yeah, good point. I'll edit it to reflect that. Thanks a bunch!

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The kids who did this to themselves were all undergrads, with maybe one of them studying chemistry, and they came up with this idea on their own (or think they did, anyway). I have no problem crosslinking it to 2498, but it should probably be more along the lines of

SCP-1475 is insistent that it and its compatriots were acting independently, but given that the neurotoxic compound displays significant similarities to materials used during the RAINBOW ASTRA program, investigations are ongoing to determine whether this is coincidence or the result of an unknown party attempting to replicate the program's results.

Basically, you need to maintain that these idiot kids were (or thought they were) doing this on their own.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Considering that RAINBOW ASTRA was co-developed by the Foundation, the Foundation would know who was involved as test subjects.

Hmm, yes, good point.

Also, considering that 1475 was found in 2013 while 2498 happened during the Cold War, perhaps 1475 is an attempt to apply the ideas behind RAINBOW ASTRA by someone else?

Hmm, also a good idea. Let me see what I can do. Thanks a bunch.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Considering that RAINBOW ASTRA was co-developed by the Foundation, the Foundation would know who was involved as test subjects.

Also, considering that 1475 was found in 2013 while 2498 happened during the Cold War, perhaps 1475 is an attempt to apply the ideas behind RAINBOW ASTRA by someone else?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Stopped. Edits made.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

When I read this, I was immediately reminded by SCP-2498 both in back story and their anomalous effects. I get the vibe that SCP-1475 was a failed RAINBOW BODY test subject, so that's what I'm going for.

I propose the following addition:

SCP-1475 is insistent that it and its partners were acting independently, but given that the neurotoxic compound displays significant similarities to materials used during the RAINBOW ASTRA programme, investigations are ongoing to determine whether this is coincidence or the result of an unknown party attempting to replicate the program's results.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AAAAANNDDD bringing this out of a rating of +100 😱

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Which is also patently false. Much of our brain's processing power goes into maintaining our own autonomic functions and sensory functions. The cerebellum contains half of the brain's neurons and its role is strictly coordination for tasks as mundane as walking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or is was just a 1/1,000,000 chance. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Another typo needs fixing.

Emporer Penguins

Should be

Emperor Penguins

The e & o were swapped. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's also a misconception. People usually don't pay careful attention to the wording, when in reality, the full statement is "You only use 10% if your brain at any one time".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll be linking to this every time someone mentions that trope from now on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I immediately thought of "you are now breathing manually" trolls as well. I wish I could temporarily induce this in them in return!

Too mean?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, that's brilliant and terrifying. In many ways, this is one of the most terrifying SCPs on the site, because I can't imagine 100-Montauk, or being ripped apart in a pocket dimension, or being turned into a zombie… but I can imagine desperately having to focus on everything going on in your body just to stay alive.

Agh, my god, it's making me shudder just thinking about it. The whole 'breathing manually' thing was bad enough.

Take my +1 and never always bother me like this again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the mocking of the 10% of the brain trope, and I love that you took it so much more than just making fun of that trope. +1

item.title: Excellent!
item.content:encoded: This rocked in every way that an SCP needs to rock. You nailed the clinical tone, and created a truly horrifying downside. "I'm sorry, but I can't talk, chew gum, and operate my endocrine system at the same time, hold on."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Have a +1. But be warned, you can only use 10% of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, keep in mind that they FAILED in their attempt. Of the 5 participants, 4 died within hours due to massive seizures, and every attempt that the Foundation has made to replicate the stuff has been just as lethal. There's something specifically about this guy that allowed him to react to the neurotoxin so as to poorly mimic the intended results.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love it. Love everything about it. This is just solid gold execution of a pretty good idea.

oh, one tiny typo

SCP is to be kept

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And everyone sane knows that playing russian roulette by whacking an anti-tank mine with a hammer is stupid , yet three drunk Cambodian dudes apparently did it. And don't think scientists are immune to having dumb ideas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a massive retard but even I know that the whole "we only use 10% of our brains" thing is a joke. I'd prefer to think that anyone with the brainpower to create an SCP would also know this. Downvote from me on the simple premise of the SCP, although the tone etc. was good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it. Wonderful moral in all of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, but who said that these were scientists? My idea is that they were freshmen/sophomores (maybe one was a grad student who got them into the lab after hours) who were maybe a little high when they conceived and executed this whole thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh.
Scientists and engineers aren't immune to darwin award inducing stupidity either. I know because I are one =^.^=

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It also irritates me for this reason as well, because dammit those scientists should have known that, but at the same time I really like the idea of this guy's condition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well-written, interesting and plausible.
I'll be using this to show some of my friends a great example of a neurological SCP, if you don't mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like humanoid SCPs that really show another way to "Protect" — the Foundation doesn't need to protect anyone from this guy, but they're the only ones who have a chance of protecting him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, Drew. Still not clicking for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ho. Lee. Balls.

A humanoid SCP that's good!

And it makes sense.

Take them, take all my upvotes!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh. There's nothing of interest or value to this besides trying to deconstruct a trope that's already well on its way to being discredited, and it doesn't do it in an interesting way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked the pacing - about 3/4 in I had an ohhhhhh moment. Also, liked the touch of the individual not being Caucasian, simply because so many of them are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh dear God this is horrifying.

…and fucking awesome.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I grasp that this is an attempt at parodying the "we only use 10% of our brains!" myth, but it just doesn't work for me for all the same reasons that that actual myth fails.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, 'twas a typo. I feex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Typo fixed. Thanks for pointing it out.

(Emporer penguins, I hope)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
research on SCP-1475 is targetted

Should that be "targeted" or what? It just doesn't look right to me as-is but I'm not sure if it's correct so I don't want to go edit it right away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 brain power!

Minor typo:

have the follow medical monitoring

should be "following".

(According to Douglas Adams, the remaining 90% is used to store penguins.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I only somewhat like the concept, but the execution is perfect. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: good one. I like it a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An excellent deconstruction of that stupid trope. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel your pain, and soothe it with a +1 x3

Seriously, good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's finally here! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because that whole "we only use 10% of our brains" trope pisses me off so much.
